rulings.Case Title: WALTER DODSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'S MOTION To Transfer Venue IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL L PRICE VS. NIKOLAI TEHIN JR et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 2. PLAINTIFF CAROL PRICE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 1, 2007. OPPOSING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES ALONG WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND ALLEN et al VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT A.B.B., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES ARE FACTUALLY DEVOID. THE GOULD REPORT IS STRUCK AS IT LACKS AUTHENTICATION, IS HEARSAY AND ILLEGIBLE. GIVEN THAT THIS CASE HAS BEEN PENDING SINCE OCTOBER 2005, THERE IS NOT A BASIS FOR A CONTINUANCE, PARTICULARLY GIVEN A TRIAL DATE OF SEPTEMBER 24, 2007. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND ALLEN et al VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT MOTOROLA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE IS NOT FACTUALLY DEVOID. PLAINTIFF IDENTIFIED DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS AND THERE IS EVIDENCE THAT CERTAIN OF THOSE PRODUCTS CONTAINED ASBESTOS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LUCILLE SAPIENZA et al VS. AMERICAN STANDARD, INC., INDIVIDUALLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANT SUPRO CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED FOR THE REASONS STATED WITH RESPECT TO THE DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/DP-X)

rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSE, L.L.C. VS. LCD INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 7. DEFENDANT L.C.D. INCORPORATED, RICHARD HANSEN AND L.C.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT: COURT DENIES MOTION TO STRIKE TREBLE DAMAGES. C.C.P. SECTION 1029.8 AUTHORIZES SUCH DAMAGES. STRIKE B. & P. SECTION 7160 AS A BASIS FOR RECOVERY. DENY AS TO ATTORNEY'S FEES BASED ON C.C.P. SECTION 1029.8 AND C.C.P. SECTION 1021.5. DENY MOTION TO STRIKE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SEVERAL BASIS FOR FUTURE AND SPECIAL DAMAGES. GRANT AS TO INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. DENY AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSE, L.L.C. VS. LCD INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 7. DEFENDANTS L.C.D. INCORPORATED, RICHARD HANSEN AND L.C.D.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. FACTS ARE ALLEGED TO SUPPORT SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. B. & P. CODE SECTION 7031 (B), 7028.4 CREATE A PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION. B. & P. CODE SECTION 5536 AND C.C.P SECTION 1029.8 PROVIDE A BASIS FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED A B. & P. SECTION 17200 CLAIM. =(302/PJM/AY)
